user_message: Christopher Martinez at Parnell Aerospace just sent this. Help me pull the context.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 12:54 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for escalation triage. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 12:36 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Parnell Aerospace

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 12:08 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-B trap (Skips stakeholder-verification step); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-B failure mode (Skips stakeholder-verification step)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-B -- Skips stakeholder-verification step
